channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Monday.com aims to be an ‘AI-First’ platform with latest enhancements
item.description: Monday.com has accelerated its push into artificial intelligence (AI) with the announcement this week of its AI vision, which includes three areas of focus: AI Blocks, embedded Product Power-ups, and a forthcoming Digital Workforce of AI agents.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: DOGE has disregarded data protection and privacy norms. The consequences will be felt years down the line
item.description: It has been a tumultuous few weeks since Donald Trump took office for the second time as president of the United States, While Trump has garnered headlines for his outlandish executive orders aimed at recasting the country in his own shape, Elon Musk, the tech billionaire turned government quango-bu
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: How to strengthen the security of Oracle applications without relying on OAU
item.description: Vendor support agreements have long been a sticking point for customers, and the Oracle Applications Unlimited (OAU) program is no different.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Paris AI Action Summit: US and UK refuse to sign accord
item.description: The escalating electricity demands of artificial intelligence systems are raising concerns about the technology’s sustainability — but that’s apparently of little concern to the governments of the US and the UK.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Inside SoftBank’s plans for Stargate and OpenAI
item.description: Masayoshi Son is back on top. On January 22, President Donald Trump announced a joint venture from Son’s investment holding company, SoftBank, along with OpenAI and Oracle, to invest billions of dollars in AI infrastructure projects in the U.S. The project, called Stargate, will be chaired by Son.
item.dc:creator: Yasmin Gagne, Fast Company

item.title: OpenAI CEO Rejects $97 Billion Takeover Bid Led by Elon Musk
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has rejected a $97 billion takeover bid led by rival Elon Musk, but the unsolicited offer could complicate Altman's push to transform the maker of ChatGPT into a for-profit company.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Thalia Beaty And Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Samsung HBM roadmap shows Google could become Nvidia's fiercest competitor in AI by 2026, but I wonder what's happening to Microsoft
item.description: Samsung’s HBM roadmap shows Nvidia is the top buyer of high-bandwidth memory, followed by Google and Amazon.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Russian cybercrime network targeted for sanctions across US, UK and Australia
item.description: The U.S., U.K. and Australia have sanctioned a Russian web-hosting services provider and two Russian men who administer the service in support of Russian ransomware syndicate LockBit.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: All your messages in one place: the new Besties app for Google Pixel will bring together chats from Google Messages, WhatsApp, and other apps
item.description: Google is adding its new Besties app to Pixel phones, allowing users to centralize messages from their closest friends across various messaging services
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: France Aims High with One Gigawatt Nuclear Power for AI Infrastructure
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron announces €109 billion for private investments in AI infrastructure, powered by one gigawatt of nuclear energy.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Ukraine's rare earth elements could help keep US military aid flowing
item.description: Ukraine has offered to strike a deal with U.S. President Donald Trump for continued American military aid in exchange for developing Ukraine’s mineral industry.
item.dc:creator: Samya Kullab, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: DeepSeek banned from NY state government devices
item.description: New York State is prohibiting the China-based artificial intelligence application DeepSeek from government devices. New York Gov.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Apple’s upcoming iPhone SE 4 likely to offer major improvements
item.description: IT purchasers might be interested to learn that Apple is preparing to introduce a new entry-level and more affordable next edition iPhone SE. What sets this iteration apart is that it should be powerful enough to run Apple Intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Ye's X account deleted after antisemitic posts
item.description: Ye, the rapper formerly known as Kanye West, has left the social media platform X after a days-long posting spree that included multiple antisemitic comments.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft Teams is becoming a prime target for sophisticated scammers
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says hackers are using Microsoft Teams for phishing, vishing and quishing via social engineering.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft Teams is becoming more like Facebook
item.description: Microsoft Teams users can expect a slew of new features in an upcoming update that is currently in full testing, including Storyline, a Facebook-like feed where users can share messages, news, or congratulations.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Beleaguered Cal State University’s $17 million artificial intelligence initiative defended, attacked
item.description: The California State University system, facing an estimated budget shortfall of $428 million to $1 billion next year because of state-level cuts and deferments, this week announced a partnership with major technology companies including OpenAI, Google and Microsoft to bring their artificial intellig
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Latino workers working to overcome a technological divide brought on by automation, AI
item.description: As jobs become more reliant on technology some Latino workers can be left behind due to a lack of digital skills exacerbated by a lack of accessibility.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Huge healthcare data breach exposes over 1 million Americans' sensitive information
item.description: Community Health Center, a Connecticut-based healthcare provider, has disclosed a recent data breach affecting over 1 million people in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Bill would ban DeepSeek
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How the slash-and-burn tactics Musk brought to Washington backfired at Twitter
item.description: When Elon Musk bought Twitter in 2022, he laid off thousands of employees, stopped paying rent and auctioned off coffee makers and office chairs in hopes of a big turnaround.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Amazon to pay nearly $4M to settle lawsuit alleging it took tips from drivers
item.description: Amazon has agreed to pay nearly $4 million to settle charges that the e-commerce company subsidized its labor costs by taking tips its delivery drivers received from customers, District of Columbia Attorney General Brian L. Schwalb said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Robot dog sprints into history books by breaking speed records
item.description: A Chinese startup has developed a robot, Mirror Me, that has run about 100 meters in under 10 seconds, pushing the boundaries of robotics and speed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How AI could replace government workers to reduce spending
item.description: Elon Musk and DOGE are reportedly using artificial intelligence to analyze federal spending and where cuts an be made
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: UK orders Apple to let it access everyone’s encrypted data
item.description: In its limited wisdom, the deeply unpopular UK government has decided to break privacy for the entire world, slamming Apple with a top secret order that demands blanket access to personal data. Apple must create a “back door” to enable surveillance, according to The Washington Post.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The hidden threat of neglected cloud infrastructure
item.description: In 2012, the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) issued a public advisory regarding software to monitor and control building systems.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: New law in Brazil is making students put away their smartphones at school
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian students returned to class this week with a new task: staying away from their smartphones as a new law restricting their use in schools took effect.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: GitHub Copilot previews agent mode
item.description: GitHub has added new capabilities to its GitHub Copilot AI-powered coding assistant, including a preview of a new agent mode capable of iterating on its own code. The company also announced the general availability of Copilot Edits in Visual Studio Code.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OpenAI looks across US for sites to build its Trump-backed Stargate AI data centers
item.description: OpenAI is scouring the U.S. for sites to build a network of huge data centers to power its artificial intelligence technology, expanding beyond a flagship Texas location and looking across 16 states to accelerate the Stargate project championed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Fortinet targets branch offices with upgraded firewalls
item.description: Fortinet has refreshed its branch office firewalls, adding power and features to its next-generation FortiGate G-series devices.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to block your phone from tracking your location
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Smartphones are useful tools for everyday life, but they're privy to nearly everything about you, including all the places you've been — if you let them.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Google scraps its diversity hiring goals as it complies with Trump's new government contractor rules
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google is scrapping some of its diversity hiring targets, joining a lengthening list of U.S. companies that have abandoned or scaled back their diversity, equity and inclusion programs.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOGE team seeks access to other agency systems after getting Treasury data
item.description: A Treasury official says DOGE team members have “read-only” access to the data from its payment systems as part of an “operational efficiency assessment." The post DOGE team seeks access to other agency systems after getting Treasury data first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Digma Preemptive Observability Analysis engine tackles AI code bugs
item.description: Digma has unveiled Preemptive Observability Analysis, an observability engine designed to reduce bugs introduced by AI code generation and other coding issues. The commercial version, introduced February 5, follows a limited-capability free version.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Fast fashion, laptops and toys are likely to cost more due to US tariffs on Chinese imports
item.description: A sweeping new U.S. tariff on products made in China is expected to increase the prices American consumers pay for a wide array of products, from the ultra-cheap apparel sold on online shopping platforms to toys and electronic devices such as computers and cellphones.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Apple lashes out at iPhone porn app maker and the EU rules allowing its download
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple has chided a newly introduced pornography app available in the European Union, warning that the bloc’s digital rules opening the way for third-party app store downloads undermines consumer confidence in the tech giant.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation
